## Overwatch Report — 2026-07-08  ### 🔴 Escalation (Section 14 — surfaced repeatedly, unanswered) **Layer 4.2 promote/retire decision** (`Core/frameworks/intent/.intent/signals/SIG-2026-06-12-layer42-calibration-review.md`, +06-28, +07-03 x2): surfaced **4 times**, answered **0 times**, now 21 days old. The 2026-07-03 update executed a recall-grammar widening (1.7% to 77.1% would-block-eligible recall, adversarially verified) as a prerequisite; a fresh warn-only calibration window against the widened grammar is the only remaining gate before this decision returns with trustworthy numbers. Recommend identifying and killing stale ones. - `elgato-mcp-server`: 2 real instances + 2 disclaimer companions (normal pairing count) but **peripheral is not attached** (streamdeck=false) — per Section 0's instruction this is "orphaned plugin processes (device not attached)," not a standard duplicate. Worth a look since it's holding memory for a device that isn't connected. - `workspace-mcp`, `library-index-mcp`, `mcp-server-filesystem`, `mcp-server-github`: all at the expected 2-real-instance count. No duplication concern.  **[Root hygiene — corroborated by the 2026-07-06 audit]** - `Workspaces/Workspaces/` — a directory literally named "Workspaces" nested inside Workspaces root, containing `.context/` and `ux-audit-subaru-com/`. This looks like debris from a copy or scoping mistake, not intentional structure. - `_inbox/` — 19 items including 2024/2025 tax drafts, a Verity-facing MA Form 1 packet, and a large "scorecard material.md" (393KB). This is unfiled personal/tax content sitting outside the placement-resolver structure. - `README.md` and `CLAUDE.md` at root — both flagged by the audit's strict allowlist, though CLAUDE.md is explicitly load-bearing per WS-DDR-112 and README.md reads as a legitimate repo-intro doc. Lower-priority than the two above. - Core/ root: 3 dated ECOSYSTEM-*.md docs from 2026-05-20 (7 weeks stale as point-in-time snapshots) plus the actively-maintained PRODUCT-ACCOUNTABILITY-LEDGER.md — worth a decision on whether the ECOSYSTEM docs get versioned into a subdirectory or refreshed.  **[IAD] Tensions detected (Section 11a/11b)** - WS-DDR-114 (Quartermaster) reliability framing vs. `SIG-2026-07-06-workflow-fanout-burst-throttle`: the same rate-limiter failure recurred, second time with zero salvageable output. The 2026-07-02 fix never actually landed. - Subaru "M.A.R.S. relocked 06-16" claim vs. `SIG-2026-06-26-canon-recovery-executed`: a full-depth audit just 10 days after the relock found 34 drift findings across 6 root causes in live Jira/Confluence. The relock didn't hold; it's now repaired with durable C7/C8 lints that weren't present the first time. - Cast's `validate_yaml.py` corpus-health gate was silently broken on 229/581 files since 2026-06-09, undetected for a month until the 2026-07-07 nightly freshen surfaced it. Anything trusting that gate read false-clean for 4 weeks.  **[Dark zones]** (empty in 14-day window, active project): Turnberry, OptumCareWellMed, ASA (standby), Throughline, Warp, coherence-engineering framework, KayleighDeMello, plus ~8 lower-confidence Core/products tools (gauge, gauntlet, reckoning, lodestone, understudy, cortege, conduit, topography).  **[TCC]** brew: c-ares. npm -g: netlify-cli, vercel. pip: pip itself. codeburn: current.
